title: Linear Attention
description: An attention variant that replaces explicit softmax dot-product attention with kernel or feature-map formulations that scale near-linearly with sequence length.
openingSummary: Linear attention is an attention variant that replaces full all-pairs softmax scoring with feature-map or recurrent formulations, trading exact dense attention for better sequence-length scaling on long inputs.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Linear attention is an attention variant that reformulates attention without building the full softmax score matrix. Feature maps on queries and keys let the model accumulate key-value statistics in linear time rather than scoring every query-key pair explicitly.

whyItExists.title: Why It Exists
whyItExists.body: Linear attention targets sequence-length scaling, attention compute cost, and memory bandwidth when dense quadratic attention would dominate runtime on long contexts.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: Queries and keys pass through a feature map φ that makes their product associative. The model accumulates φ(K)ᵀV across positions and combines the result with φ(Q) at each step, avoiding explicit materialization of the full n-by-n attention matrix.

mathOrComputeSchema.title: Math Or Compute Schema
mathOrComputeSchema.body: With feature map φ applied to queries and keys, linear attention computes output through associative products rather than softmax over all pairs. The formulas below contrast dense multi-head attention against linear attention that routes through φ and a normalization term.

comparedToNearbyModules.title: Compared To Nearby Modules
comparedToNearbyModules.body: Compared with multi-head attention, linear attention changes the compute mechanism rather than only sharing key-value heads. Multi-query and grouped-query attention remain dense over allowed positions but reduce key-value head count. Linear attention instead pursues subquadratic sequence scaling through kernel or recurrence formulations.

exampleArchitectures.title: Example Architectures
exampleArchitectures.body: Linear attention appears in long-context models and research architectures that combine subquadratic attention layers with standard dense blocks to balance efficiency and expressiveness.

limitationsAndTradeoffs.title: Limitations And Tradeoffs
limitationsAndTradeoffs.body: Feature-map linear attention is not identical to softmax dot-product attention. Approximation quality depends on the chosen kernel, and some formulations sacrifice the exact pairwise scoring that dense attention provides.

whyItStillMatters.title: Why It Still Matters
whyItStillMatters.body: As context lengths grow, subquadratic attention options remain a practical design axis for models that must serve long sequences without quadratic attention cost on every layer.

computeFlow.alt: Dense multi-head attention versus linear attention feature-map flow

linear.sequenceScaling: Near-linear O(n) per head with fixed feature-map dimension
linear.computeMechanism: Feature-map φ on queries and keys; associative key-value accumulation
linear.expressiveness: Approximates softmax dot-product attention via chosen kernel

mha.computeMechanism: Explicit softmax over all query-key dot products

mqa.sequenceScaling: Quadratic O(n²) with fewer key-value projections
mqa.computeMechanism: Dense softmax with shared key-value heads
mqa.expressiveness: Full pairwise softmax attention with shared key-value heads

gqa.sequenceScaling: Quadratic O(n²) with reduced key-value head count
gqa.computeMechanism: Dense softmax with grouped key-value heads
gqa.expressiveness: Full pairwise softmax attention with grouped key-value heads

g.definition: Feature map applied to queries and keys to enable associative accumulation.

gi.definition: Normalization denominator from summed feature-mapped keys.

mhaKvHeads.label: H key-value head pairs (1:1 with queries)

mhaOneToOne.label: Each query attends to every key position

linearFeatureMap.label: Feature map φ on queries and keys

linearAssociativeUpdate.label: Associative key-value accumulation without full score matrix

linearAssociativeNote.label: Queries read from running summaries instead of scoring every key
